DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AS TO THE DEC-05-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JHP CAPITAL PARTNERS, L.P.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO AND REQUEST MONETARY SANCTIONS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. AS WITH LINE 1, THE MOTION IS MOOT EXCEPT AS TO SANCTIONS, WHICH ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,200. SANCTIONS ARE FOR REASONABLE EXPENSES INCURRED IN RESEARCH AND PREPARATION OF THE MOTION AND COURT TIME. GHANOONI V SUPER SHUTTLE (1993) 20 CA 4TH 256, 262. COUNSEL LAURA GREALISH IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, AND EMAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM . JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. =(525/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. LAURA GREALISH, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (tel: 415-788-1900) for Plaintiff.
Atty. ROGER JACKSON, Esq. appearing along with Atty. CLAY WILKINSON, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of Fernald & Zaffos APC (tel: 323-410-0320) for Defendant Daniel Patterson.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JHP CAPITAL PARTNERS, L.P.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant contests tentative ruling. Counsel for the plaintiffs does not stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen. Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion of oral argument, Counsel Clay Wilkinson states the defendant stipulates to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen. Both counsel sign the stipulation. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion is granted. Defendant shall produce code compliant responses within 10 days. The parties shall enter into a reasonable protective order.
Sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $1,800.

Counsel Laura Grealish is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, and email it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com .

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(525/JPT)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-29-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MICHAEL BRADLEY, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) for Plaintiff.
Atty. BRANDON FERNALD, Esq. of Fernald & Zaffos APC (Tel: 323-410-0320) for Defendant Daniel Patterson.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT DANIEL PATTERSON's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to strike is denied. The allegations regarding the parties' prior negotiations add contextual background and are not clearly "irrelevant, false, or improper" within the meaning of CCP 436(a). (See also CCP 452 [liberally construe pleading].) The punitive damages allegations are well-pleaded.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
